quarantine_items.title: How new FAA rules are opening the skies to personal eVTOLs
quarantine_items.summary: Imagine operating a personal VTOL from your driveway and transitioning seamlessly into open skies. With the FAA’s Modernization of Special Airworthiness Certification ruling, the dream of taking off from your home, avoiding traffic and flying directly to your destination is a step closer to reality. The MOSAIC rule changes how VTOLs can be certified for personal use. The ruling, finalized in July last year, removes older maximum weight limits for Light Sport Aircraft (LSA) in favor of performance-based metrics, paving the way for owner-operated VTOLs. Continue reading How new FAA rules are opening the skies to personal eVTOLs at Aerospace Testing International.
